untranslatable_reason: This signal is fundamentally EVENT-DRIVEN and requires external data inputs (catalyst event dates, outcome probability assessments from polling/structural data, policy platform analysis, and event confirmation status) that are NOT available in the allowed technical indicators list. The signal depends on: (1) forward-looking catalyst event scheduling (not a price/volume/technical indicator), (2) probabilistic assessments external to market data (polling, structural analysis), (3) policy/political platform content analysis, and (4) real-time event outcome confirmation. These are macro/fundamental/external data sources, not tradeable technical signals. A backtest engine limited to price, volume, and momentum indicators cannot execute this rule without external event data feeds.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_0 describes a catalyst-driven trading approach requiring real-time monitoring of structural events and their confirmation status. This is a discretionary fundamental strategy unsuitable for rule-based technical backtesting. To make this backtestable, you would need: (1) an external event calendar data source, (2) probability scores derived from polling APIs or macro data feeds, and (3) event outcome feeds. The parameters provided (catalyst_lead_time_weeks: 26, confidence_threshold_pct: 80) suggest a framework, but without the ability to query whether a catalyst event exists, its probability, and its confirmation status, no technical condition set can replicate this strategy.
